name: Xilonen
description: Xilonen, also known as Chicomecóatl ('Seven Serpent'), was a major Aztec goddess associated with young maize, sustenance, and agriculture. She was a vital figure in the Aztec pantheon, representing the life-giving power of the earth and the promise of a bountiful harvest. Her name, Xilonen, translates to 'tender maize cob,' directly linking her to the young, unripe corn that formed a crucial part of the Aztec diet. As Chicomecóatl, she was also linked to the number seven, a sacred number in Aztec cosmology often associated with abundance and fertility. Xilonen was venerated during the Huey Tozoztli festival, a time dedicated to seeking a good harvest. Offerings, including food and sacrifices, were made to her to ensure the continued growth and prosperity of the maize crop. She was often depicted with red paint on her face and carrying ears of corn, symbolizing her role as the provider of sustenance. Xilonen's importance stemmed from the central role of maize in Aztec society and culture. She embodied the hope for a successful harvest and the continuation of life, making her a beloved and respected deity. Her influence extended beyond agriculture, encompassing themes of motherhood, nourishment, and the cyclical nature of life and death. She ensured the continuation of the Aztec people through the provision of food and sustenance. Temples were built in her honor, and elaborate rituals were performed to appease her and secure her blessings. Xilonen was a powerful symbol of fertility and the life-sustaining power of the earth.
subtitle: Goddess of Young Maize and Sustenance

keyMyths.title: The Gift of Maize
keyMyths.description: Xilonen, through her divine intervention, ensures a plentiful maize harvest for the Aztec people, preventing famine. This act highlights her role as a benevolent provider.

keyMyths.title: The Seven Serpent Aspect
keyMyths.description: As Chicomecóatl, Xilonen embodies abundance and fertility, represented by the seven serpents. This connection signifies her power over the cyclical nature of life.

keyMyths.title: The Huey Tozoztli Festival
keyMyths.description: During this festival, Xilonen is honored with offerings and rituals to ensure a successful harvest. This event demonstrates the importance of agriculture in Aztec society.
